news.t: The end of freedom of the seas: Why global shipping may never be the same
Shipping ETFs on pace for best day in nearly a month after U.S., U.K. strike on Houthi rebels in Yemen stirs up more geopolitical worries
Shipping ETFs extend rally as ocean carriers remain on edge amid Houthi militant attacks in Red Sea

holdings.infoBox: BOAT is an equity ETF with a total of 48 individual holdings. The top holdings are Frontline stock at 6.62%, Matson at 5.36%, SITC International Holdings Company at 4.80%, Mitsui O.S.K. Lines at 4.70%, and A.P. Møller - Mærsk at 4.68%.

overview.description: The SonicShares Global Shipping ETF (BOAT) is an exchange-traded fund that is based on the Solactive Global Shipping index, a modified market-cap-weighted index of globally-listed companies engaged in maritime shipping. BOAT was launched on Aug 3, 2021 and is issued by Tidal.

data.title: The end of freedom of the seas: Why global shipping may never be the same
data.text: Freedom of maritime navigation has long been considered a staple feature of the post-war liberal order but all this is changing in the era of competing superpowers

data.title: Shipping ETFs on pace for best day in nearly a month after U.S., U.K. strike on Houthi rebels in Yemen stirs up more geopolitical worries

data.text: Exchange-traded funds that track U.S. shipping stocks continued to surge on Tuesday after the world's major container shippers rerouted their tankers to avoid sailing through the Red Sea following att...
